title: Creating Strong Passwords
drDigitalIntro:
A strong password is your first line of defense.
• Make it long — at least 12 characters.
• Mix uppercase, lowercase, numbers, and symbols.
• Avoid your name or birthday, and use a different one for each account.

steps.say: Type a weak password like 'password123' and see the strength meter.
Check the strength — it's weak. Click Check Strength.
Now type a strong password with letters, numbers, and symbols.
Check the strength again — see the difference!

drDigitalSuccess: You tested password strength! Long passwords with mixed characters are much harder to guess.
drDigitalHint: Type in the password field, then click Check Strength to see how secure it is.
